$schema_comment: chdb-node skip list for @clickhouse/client's integration suite. AUTHORITATIVE blacklist applied by tests/clickhouse-js/runner.mjs when running @clickhouse/client's tests with ChdbConnection injected. Format: skipFiles is per-file (entire suite of tests in the file is excluded — for files where chdb does not implement the capability the file exercises); skipTests is per-case for cases inside an otherwise-runnable file (the runner patches the file in place to convert it(...) → it.skip(...) before vitest collects, and restores after). reasons documents WHY each entry is here. Maintained per @clickhouse/client release; chdb-node refreshes the list when upstream cuts a new version.

syncedAgainst.policy: While the createClient({ connection }) injection PR is open: ShawnChen-Sirius fork branch. After merge: ClickHouse/clickhouse-js main. After next release: latest released tag (CURRENT — @clickhouse/client 1.23.0 was published to npm on 2026-06-29 as GitHub tag `client-1.23.0` at commit 70ad405, the first stable release containing the `connection` injection point from PR #879/#880). NOTE on Vitest layout: this release was cut BEFORE PR #931 (which merged 2026-06-30 as commit 199f9946 and embedded the Vitest config/setup into `packages/client-node/`). The `client-1.23.0` tag therefore still has the root-level `vitest.node.setup.ts` / `vitest.node.config.ts` and the root `test:node:integration` script — runner.mjs auto-detects and adapts to either layout. Spec paths in skipFiles/skipTests below are package-rooted (`packages/{client-common,client-node}/__tests__/...`) and unchanged across both layouts.

skipFiles.reason: HTTP transport plumbing: slow-server / dropped-connection / eager-socket-destroy scenarios exist only on the wire.
HTTP socket-pool behavior; no analogue in-process.
HTTP keep-alive; no socket pool in-process.
HTTP keep-alive header inspection; no HTTP transport.
HTTP connection pool sizing; no socket pool in-process.
HTTP agent injection; no HTTP transport in-process.
Tests the HTTP /ping endpoint; chdb's ping is SELECT 1 (already covered by the Connection contract suite in test/v3/connection/).
HTTP request/response body compression; no wire to compress in-process.
HTTP-request logger surface; in-process backend has no HTTP request to log.
Asserts X-ClickHouse-Summary HTTP response header; chdb synthesizes summary fields but has no HTTP headers.
JWT auth; chdb has no auth layer.
TLS transport; no transport security layer in-process.
Username/password authentication; chdb has no auth layer.
RBAC roles; chdb has no users/roles.
RBAC read-only user enforcement; chdb has no users.
Reads from system.query_log; chdb has no server runtime / query_log table.
HTTP multipart body for parameter binding; no HTTP transport.
Multiple parallel clients against shared cluster topology; chdb is one in-process engine, not a cluster.
HTTP session_id semantics; chdb has no HTTP session concept (persistence is the chdb Session.path).
HTTP request-body compression; no wire to compress.
HTTP response-body compression; no wire to compress.
X-ClickHouse-Exception HTTP response header; chdb maps errors via JS exceptions, not HTTP headers.
AbortSignal cancellation semantics differ: chdb single-shot abort rejects early while the underlying computation runs to completion (the C ABI has no interrupt). Documented divergence.
JSONEachRowWithProgress emits HTTP X-ClickHouse-Progress events interleaved with rows — chdb returns plain JSONEachRow output without the progress envelope.

Iterates the live system.settings catalog and tries to SET each one. chdb advertises a different set than the server (engine-only subset; some HTTP-specific entries don't exist; some chdb-specific entries are extras the test doesn't expect).

Same AbortSignal divergence as `client-common/abort_request.test.ts` — chdb's single-shot abort rejects early while the underlying computation runs to completion (C ABI has no interrupt). The client-node variant additionally exercises HTTP wire-level cancel timing that has no analogue in-process.

chdb's `query()` materializes results eagerly (one-shot Readable from a buffered result); errors surface at the awaited Promise rather than mid-stream. Tests here assert HTTP-style mid-stream error delivery which the embedded backend can't reproduce. Pinned by `test/v3/connection/contract.test.ts > query() materialization invariant`.

skipTests.test: insert > inserts values using JSON format and get the response headers
skipTests.reason: Asserts response_headers['Content-Type']; chdb has no HTTP response headers (synthesized as {}).

skipTests.test: exec and command > should get the response headers with command
skipTests.reason: Asserts HTTP response headers; chdb has none.

skipTests.test: exec and command > should get the response headers with exec
skipTests.reason: Asserts HTTP response headers; chdb has none.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] command > ignore error response > should not throw an error when ignore_error_response is true
skipTests.reason: ignore_error_response gates HTTP non-200 responses; chdb errors are typed JS throws, not HTTP responses.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] command > ignore error response > should throw an error by default when ignore_error_response is not set
skipTests.reason: Same; HTTP-specific.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] command > ignore error response > should throw an error when ignore_error_response is false
skipTests.reason: Same; HTTP-specific.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] exec > disabled stream decompression > should force decompress in case of an error
skipTests.reason: HTTP wire-level stream decompression; chdb has no compressed wire.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] exec > disabled stream decompression > should get a compressed response stream without decompressing it
skipTests.reason: HTTP wire-level stream decompression.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] exec > ignore error response > should get a compressed response stream if ignore_error_response is true and decompression is disabled
skipTests.reason: HTTP wire-level stream decompression + non-200 handling.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] exec > ignore error response > should get a decompressed response stream if ignore_error_response is true and default decompression config is passed
skipTests.reason: HTTP wire-level stream decompression + non-200 handling.

skipTests.test: config > should set request timeout with "request_timeout" setting
skipTests.reason: HTTP socket-level request_timeout; no HTTP socket in-process.

skipTests.test: config > should specify the default database name on creation
skipTests.reason: Uses `SELECT * FROM numbers LIMIT 2` (no parens) which only works against the server's `numbers` table-function shorthand; chdb requires `numbers(N)` argument form. Test isn't asserting chdb-supported behavior.

skipTests.test: data types > should work with floating point types
skipTests.reason: Float64 output precision divergence: chdb emits 17 significant digits for full IEEE 754 round-trip safety (e.g. -0.11325939434432401), clickhouse-server emits 15 (-0.113259394344324). Same numeric value, different string representation. Round-trip through chdb is lossless; the test asserts string-equality of the original fixture.

skipTests.test: data types > Dates > should work with JS Date objects
skipTests.reason: chdb resolves DateTime[64] without an explicit TZ argument against the system timezone (libchdb captures ICU at load time and `SET session_timezone='UTC'` does not influence the output formatter); clickhouse-server defaults to UTC in CI. Round-trip is lossless at the instant level — display string differs by the host's UTC offset. Same divergence underlies the other 4 datetime skipTests below.

skipTests.test: DateTime > DateTime > should insert DateTime and get it back
skipTests.reason: Same chdb default-timezone divergence (DateTime values display in the host's local TZ, not UTC).

skipTests.test: DateTime > DateTime64(3) > should insert DateTime64(3) and get it back
skipTests.reason: Same chdb default-timezone divergence.

skipTests.test: select with query binding > Date(Time) > handles DateTime in a parameterized query
skipTests.reason: Same chdb default-timezone divergence.

skipTests.test: select with query binding > Date(Time) > handles DateTime64(3) in a parameterized query
skipTests.reason: Same chdb default-timezone divergence.

skipTests.test: select > returns an error details provided by ClickHouse
skipTests.reason: Error-type parsing divergence: chdb's error string is `Code: 62. DB::Exception: Syntax error: failed at position 16 (JSON) (line 2, col 8): JSON. Expected end of query. (SYNTAX_ERROR)`. `parseError` in @clickhouse/client-common picks the first parenthesized token as `type`, so it extracts `JSON` instead of `SYNTAX_ERROR`. Same numeric code (62), same message content; only the parsed `type` field differs.

skipTests.test: select > should get the response headers
skipTests.reason: Asserts HTTP response headers; chdb has none.

skipTests.test: data types > Nested > should work with nested (input_format_import_nested_json = 1)
skipTests.reason: Nested type with the HTTP-side `input_format_import_nested_json=1` setting; chdb's nested-JSON input path has a different default behavior. Not a chdb engine gap per se (Nested in general works), but the specific JSON-input setting interaction differs from server.

skipTests.test: [Node.js] streaming e2e > should stream a Parquet file
skipTests.reason: Streams a Parquet file into chdb via `INSERT FORMAT Parquet`; chdb's `ParquetV3BlockInputFormat` rejects this specific fixture with `apache::thrift::protocol::TProtocolException: invalid TType`. The server's older Parquet input format accepts it. Engine-version divergence in Parquet decoder.
